arts.ttl: Rushian Hepburn-Murphy double helps ease Crawley drop fears
Exeter City 'lack killer touch' but League One safety secured
arts.sum: The Grecians thought they won it late on when Andrew Oluwabori seemingly poked home a winner, only for a late offside flag to deny him. But it finished goalless – and Bristol Rovers’ failure to win later in the day at Wrexham meant City would be playing League One football for a fourth successive season.

arts.ttl: Alex Hartridge playing his best football in an Exeter City shirt
arts.sum: It was a 16 th clean sheet of the season on an afternoon where defensively Exeter weren’t tested, but the conditions meant it was tough to go and get that winner on a challenging afternoon of football, where the conditions and the occasion called for concentration, grit, and discipline rather than flair. Exeter City defender Alex Hartridge admitted that the wet weather at St James Park affected the Grecians as they were held to a goalless draw by Burton Albion.
